# Copilot Instructions for tokenization-rwa

This repository is a small Foundry-based Solidity project.  The `stakeContract.sol` in `src/` is intended to become the heart
of a real‑world asset tokenization/staking system, but for now the codebase is largely the default
counter sample copied from the Foundry quick‑start.  Use the comments below to orient AI agents to the
structure, conventions, and developer workflows that make the repo build and testable.

---

## 🧱 Project Layout

- **`src/`** – all production Solidity contracts live here.  At the moment there is only one file,
  `stakeContract.sol` (currently empty) and sample code from the default counter exercise.
- **`test/`** – Solidity unit tests.  Tests use `forge-std/Test.sol` and follow the `Counter.t.sol`
  template (`setUp()`, `test_…`, `testFuzz_…`).  Additional tests you add for `stakeContract.sol`
  should be placed here.
- **`script/`** – Forge deployment scripts written in Solidity.  The existing `Counter.s.sol`
  example is commented out; adapt this pattern for any on‑chain deployment automation.
- **`lib/forge-std/`** – vendored standard library used by tests and scripts.  Do not modify,
  treat it as a dependency like any other external import.
- **`foundry.toml`** – configuration for forge (compiler version `^0.8.13`, etc.).

Other conventional directories such as `cache/` are generated by Foundry and can be ignored.

## 🚀 Common Workflows

These commands are expected to succeed from the workspace root and are the preferred way
for developers (and agents) to interact with the project:

```bash
# compile contracts
forge build

# run the full suite of tests (includes fuzz tests)
forge test

# format all Solidity files according to the project's style
forge fmt

# capture gas usage snapshots (used in CI)
forge snapshot

# run a local node for manual interaction
anvil

# deploy a contract with a script (example shown in README)
forge script script/Counter.s.sol:CounterScript --rpc-url $RPC --private-key $PK
```

> **Note:** The README.md contains a quick reference for the above commands and should be
> consulted before writing new instructions.

## 📐 Coding Conventions and Patterns

- Every Solidity file begins with an SPDX license comment and `pragma solidity ^0.8.13;`.
- Use `import {X} from "forge-std/…"` for testing and scripting utilities; the imports
  resolve against the `lib/forge-std` subdirectory.
- Unit tests are written in Solidity.  Naming conventions:
  - `setUp()` to deploy contracts and prepare state
  - `test_…` for deterministic behavior
  - `testFuzz_…` for fuzzing inputs (the engine automatically supplies random values)

Example from `test/Counter.t.sol`:

```solidity
function test_Increment() public {
    counter.increment();
    assertEq(counter.number(), 1);
}

function testFuzz_SetNumber(uint256 x) public {
    counter.setNumber(x);
    assertEq(counter.number(), x);
}
```

- Scripts in `script/` must inherit from `Script` and call `vm.startBroadcast()` /
  `vm.stopBroadcast()` around on‑chain actions.

## 🔗 Integration Points

- The only external dependency is `forge-std`; other libraries or on‑chain interactions
  will be added as the `stakeContract.sol` logic evolves.  When importing external
  packages, update `foundry.toml` accordingly.
- Deployment targets and RPC URLs are passed via command‑line flags or environment
  variables when running `forge script`.

## 🧠 Architectural Notes

At present, the repo has one core contract (the placeholder `stakeContract.sol`).
Future development is expected to implement tokenization of real‑world assets,
stake/deposit logic, and possibly an ERC‑20/ERC‑721 wrapper.  AI agents should treat
`stakeContract.sol` as the place to add business logic and expand tests/scripts
accordingly.  For anything that isn’t defined in the repo (e.g. external token
interfaces) look at `lib/forge-std/src/interfaces` for examples or add new interface
files under `src/`.

## 🧩 Testing and Gas

- Tests are executed with `forge test`; use `--match-test` to run a subset or
  `--fork-url` when interacting with a live network.
- Gas snapshots are stored under `./gas-snapshots` (created by `forge snapshot`).
  Maintain these after major protocol changes to catch regressions.

## 📌 Tips for AI Agents

- Keep changes minimal and always run `forge fmt` and `forge test` after editing
  to ensure the workspace compiles and all tests pass.
- When adding new contracts or tests, mimic the structure of the existing counter
  example for naming and organization.
- The style is intentionally simple; avoid introducing unfamiliar dependencies or
  complex tooling unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Because `stakeContract.sol` is mostly empty, ask the user for specification details
  before implementing business logic.

---

> **Next steps:** Review this guidance and let me know if any sections are unclear
> or if there are additional patterns I missed.  I'm happy to iterate!
